food_subgroup: Fermented rice and lentil crepes with spiced potato filling

ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn FOODON_00002572 covers dosa as a general category. Butter Masala Thosai is a filled and buttered variant; no exact code exists for this specific preparation.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0EJW covers dosa as a category. The butter and masala potato filling variant is not separately coded; closest_parent applied.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT code exists for dosa or its variants in the clinical terminology system.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of Butter Masala Thosai is approximately 200 g, comprising one large fermented crepe filled with spiced potato masala and finished with a spread of butter.

nutrient_source.title: Masala Dosa — ICMR-NIN Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (2017 revised edition), composite entry for masala dosa with potato filling
nutrient_source.organization: Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: ICMR-NIN reports masala dosa (plain dosa with spiced potato filling). Butter Masala Thosai adds approximately 5 g butter per 100 g serving compared to the plain masala dosa baseline. Nutrient values have been adjusted upward for fat (+3.5 g/100 g) and energy (+32 kcal/100 g) to account for the butter component, consistent with USDA SR Legacy butter values (USDA FDC ID 173410). All other macros and micronutrients are taken directly from the ICMR-NIN masala dosa entry.

gi_source.title: Glycemic index of South Indian fermented foods: dosa and idli — Wolever TMS et al., and University of Sydney GI Database entry for dosa (fermented rice-lentil crepe)
gi_source.source_match_notes: The University of Sydney GI Database lists plain dosa (fermented rice and urad dal crepe) with a GI of approximately 55 (low). Butter Masala Thosai is a filled and buttered variant of plain dosa. The addition of potato masala filling raises the effective GI slightly, but the fat content from butter and oil moderates glycemic response. A GI of 55 is applied as the best defensible analogous estimate for this preparation, consistent with the dosa entry in the University of Sydney GI Database. Glycemic load calculated as (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (55 × 53.0) / 100 = 29.2, using 200 g serving size with 26.5 g carbohydrate per 100 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium content (~210 mg/100 g; ~420 mg per serving)
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should be aware that one serving of Butter Masala Thosai contributes approximately 420 mg sodium, representing about 18% of the 2300 mg daily limit recommended by the American Heart Association; portion control and requesting reduced salt preparation are advisable.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Moderate glycemic load (~29 per serving) from fermented rice-lentil crepe and potato masala filling
type2_diabetes.guidance: Butter Masala Thosai carries a moderate glycemic load of approximately 29 per standard 200 g serving; individuals with Type 2 Diabetes should limit portion size, pair with high-fibre vegetables or protein, and monitor postprandial glucose response, as the potato filling contributes rapidly digestible starch despite the moderating effect of fermentation and added fat.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Saturated fat from butter (~2.8 g/100 g; ~5.6 g per serving) and moderate sodium
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The butter component of Butter Masala Thosai contributes approximately 5.6 g of saturated fat per serving, which represents about 28% of the 20 g daily saturated fat limit for cardiovascular risk management; individuals with cardiovascular disease should limit frequency of consumption and consider requesting reduced-butter preparation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Butter Masala Thosai is a popular South Indian and Singaporean Indian dish consisting of a large, thin, crispy fermented crepe made from a batter of ground rice and urad dal (black gram lentils) that has been fermented overnight to develop a characteristic mild sourness. The crepe is cooked on a hot griddle until golden and crisp, then filled with a spiced potato masala — a mixture of mashed or roughly crushed boiled potatoes sautéed with onions, mustard seeds, curry leaves, green chilli, and turmeric — and finished with a generous spread of butter on the surface, which gives the dish its distinctive richness and golden sheen. The texture contrasts a crisp, lacy exterior with a soft, aromatic filling. In Singapore, Butter Masala Thosai is a staple breakfast and tiffin item served at Indian coffee shops (kopitiams) and hawker stalls, typically accompanied by coconut chutney and sambar (a lentil-based vegetable soup). Nutritionally, it provides moderate energy (~175 kcal/100 g), carbohydrates from the rice-lentil crepe and potato filling, and fat primarily from butter and cooking oil. The fermentation process improves digestibility and bioavailability of B vitamins. Clinically, the dish carries a moderate glycemic load and moderate sodium content, making portion awareness important for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease. The dish is vegetarian and halal-suitable.
common_user_queries: How many calories in Butter Masala Thosai?
What is the GI of masala dosa?
Is Butter Masala Thosai suitable for diabetics?

Butter Masala Thosai nutrition facts Singapore

confidence_reason: Nutrient values are adapted from the ICMR-NIN masala dosa entry (an approved primary source for Indian dishes) with a calculated adjustment for the butter component using USDA SR Legacy butter values. No exact entry for Butter Masala Thosai exists in any approved primary source. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database dosa entry. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high.
notes: 1. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN masala dosa baseline with butter adjustment; not an exact match. 2. vitamin_b12_mcg is 0.0 — confirmed zero, as this is a plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients other than butter, which contributes negligible B12 at the quantities used; confirmed zero per ICMR-NIN plant-based food entries. 3. vitamin_d_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. 4. selenium_mcg is -1 as it is not reported in the ICMR-NIN source database for this food category. 5. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, and trans_fat_g are estimated from the combined fatty acid profile of cooking oil and butter using USDA SR Legacy reference values; these are approximate. 6. GI of 55 applied as analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database dosa entry; gi_category is low per the ≤55 threshold rule. 7. Glycemic load per serving = (55 × 53.0 g carbohydrate per 200 g serving) / 100 = 29.2. 8. The cuisine_tags in the input included 'Noodles' and 'Drinks & Beverages' which appear to be platform tagging artefacts and have not been carried forward as they do not describe this dish.
